scalar.name: Scalar
scalar.short_description: Scalar builds API interfaces: hosted docs, an open-source API client, SDK generation, and an API registry.

features.description: Write documentation using Markdown, MDX, OpenAPI, and AsyncAPI, with two-way Git synchronization and support for custom HTML/CSS/JS.
features.title: API Docs

features.description: API Docs include AI/MCP chat capabilities for interacting with documentation.
features.title: AI/MCP chat

features.description: Generates type-safe client libraries from OpenAPI documents in TypeScript, Python, Golang, PHP, Java, and Ruby.

features.title: API Registry

features.description: Pro plan includes hosted MCP servers and automated GitHub workflows.

pros: Free plan includes hosted OpenAPI docs, a built-in API client, and unlimited viewer seats
API Client is open-source and offline-first with no vendor lock-in
SDK generation covers six languages (TypeScript, Python, Golang, PHP, Java, Ruby)
Two-way Git synchronization for documentation content
Enterprise plan offers SSO/SAML, RBAC, and SLA-backed priority support
cons: Pro plan is $72/month plus $100/month per additional individual SDK language
Free plan is limited to 1 editor seat and 50 Agent Scalar credits
Enterprise pricing requires booking a demo rather than public quoting

faqs.answer: Yes, Scalar offers a Free plan with hosted OpenAPI docs, a built-in API client, 50 Agent Scalar credits, unlimited viewer seats, and 1 editor seat.
faqs.question: Is Scalar free?

faqs.answer: The Pro plan is $72/month and adds custom domains, Git Sync with Markdown/MDX, SDKs, hosted MCP servers, automated GitHub workflows, and more editor seats.
faqs.question: How much does Scalar Pro cost?

faqs.answer: Yes, Scalar's API Client is open-source, offline-first, and cross-platform (Linux, Windows, macOS).
faqs.question: Is the Scalar API Client open source?

faqs.answer: Scalar can generate type-safe SDKs in TypeScript, Python, Golang, PHP, Java, and Ruby; each additional individual SDK language costs $100/month on Pro or Enterprise.
faqs.question: What languages does Scalar generate SDKs for?

faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan (custom pricing) includes full developer portals, SSO/SAML, RBAC, priority SLA support, and SDK/MCP migration services.
faqs.question: Does Scalar have Enterprise features?

yaak.name: Yaak
yaak.short_description: Yaak is a local-first, MIT-licensed desktop API client for HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE requests.

features.description: Send HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE requests from one app.
features.title: Multi-protocol requests

features.description: Request templating with variables and template functions.
features.title: Dynamic templating

features.description: Inspect redirects, cookies, and headers for each request.
features.title: Full request debugging

features.description: Projects are stored as plain-text files with built-in Git integration for team collaboration.
features.title: Built-in Git collaboration

features.description: Import existing collections from Postman, Insomnia, OpenAPI, cURL, and Swagger.
features.title: Import from other tools

features.description: Automatically handles OAuth 1/2, AWS v4, JWT, Basic, Bearer, and API Key authentication.
features.title: Broad authentication support

features.description: Local-only data storage, secrets encrypted with ChaCha20-Poly1305 in the OS keychain, and zero telemetry.
features.title: Local-only privacy

features.description: Connect your own AI agents via the CLI or an MCP server.
features.title: Agent/MCP connectivity

pros: MIT-licensed source code that can be audited and self-built
Free for personal use with no account required
Local-first with zero telemetry and encrypted secret storage
Supports HTTP, GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket, and SSE in one app
Built-in Git collaboration using plain-text project files
cons: Prebuilt binaries require a paid commercial license for business use even though the source is MIT-licensed
Desktop-only (Mac, Windows, Linux) — no web or mobile version
No native AI features; AI usage requires connecting your own agents

faqs.answer: Yaak is free for personal use. Commercial use of the prebuilt binaries requires a paid license (annual or lifetime), even though the source code is MIT-licensed.

faqs.answer: Yes, the source code is MIT-licensed and can be audited or self-built; licensing fees apply only to the official prebuilt binaries.

faqs.answer: Yaak is available for Mac, Windows, and Linux.
faqs.question: What platforms does Yaak support?

faqs.answer: No native AI is built in, but Yaak lets you connect your own agents via its CLI or MCP server.
faqs.question: Does Yaak have built-in AI?

faqs.answer: Yaak is made by Mountain Loop Labs, founded by Greg, the creator of Insomnia (sold in 2019). Yaak launched in January 2024.
